responses.question: Are authentication tokens short lived under 24 hours?
responses.breach_relevance: Root cause of attacker persistence after token theft

responses.question: Is OAuth 2.0 or equivalent used for all service to service authentication?
responses.breach_relevance: Static tokens enabled silent lateral movement into Snowflake

responses.question: Are service account tokens rotated on a schedule of 90 days or less?
responses.breach_relevance: No rotation policy meant stolen tokens had indefinite validity

responses.question: Are authentication tokens stored in a secrets manager?
responses.breach_relevance: Token extraction from Anodot systems suggests insecure storage

responses.question: Is MFA enforced on all service accounts with access to production data warehouses?
responses.breach_relevance: Service account compromise enabled unrestricted Snowflake access

responses.question: Are tokens scoped to least privilege access only?
responses.breach_relevance: Warehouse wide access enabled exfiltration of 78.6 million records

responses.question: Is there automated alerting when tokens are used outside normal behavior patterns?
responses.breach_relevance: No behavioral alerting allowed 10 days of silent access

responses.question: Do you have a documented incident response plan for third party integration breaches?
responses.breach_relevance: Absence of IR plan contributed to 7 day notification delay

responses.question: What is your contractual SLA for notifying customers of a security incident?
responses.breach_relevance: No SLA meant Rockstar found out via dark web not from Anodot

responses.question: Do you have SIEM or anomaly detection covering your integration pipeline?
responses.breach_relevance: Silent exfiltration went undetected across multiple customer environments

responses.question: How do you detect unauthorized use of authentication tokens issued to your platform?
responses.breach_relevance: Token reuse was completely invisible to Anodot and Rockstar

responses.question: Are customers notified when connector services go offline unexpectedly?
responses.breach_relevance: April 4 outage was not communicated giving attackers a 10 day head start

responses.question: Is access to customer data warehouses scoped to minimum required schemas only?
responses.breach_relevance: Full warehouse access enabled mass record exfiltration

responses.question: Are privileged access reviews conducted quarterly?
responses.breach_relevance: Over permissioned access likely accumulated over time unchecked

responses.question: Is network access for integrations restricted by IP allowlist?
responses.breach_relevance: Stolen tokens could be used from any IP address worldwide

responses.question: Are read and write permissions separated for analytics versus operational access?
responses.breach_relevance: Analytics tool should have been read only preventing data manipulation

responses.question: Do you log all queries made to connected customer data environments?
responses.breach_relevance: No query logging meant no forensics trail after the breach

responses.question: Do you maintain an inventory of sub-processors with access to customer data?
responses.breach_relevance: Anodot pipeline was the attack surface suggesting incomplete sub-processor visibility

responses.question: Are sub-processors subject to the same security requirements as your platform?
responses.breach_relevance: Weak link in Anodot chain enabled the pivot to Rockstar

responses.question: Do you conduct annual security assessments of sub-processors?
responses.breach_relevance: Unassessed components created unknown risk that attackers exploited

responses.question: Do you hold a current SOC 2 Type II or ISO 27001 certification?
responses.breach_relevance: Third party validation would have surfaced token and access control failures

responses.question: Do you log security events?
responses.breach_relevance: No security event logging meant silent exfiltration went undetected for 10 days

responses.question: Do you employ a third party to test your infrastructure security?
responses.breach_relevance: Independent security testing would have surfaced token storage weaknesses

responses.question: Do you employ penetration testing?
responses.breach_relevance: Connector infrastructure pen test would have identified token exposure risk

responses.question: Do you have an incident response team?
responses.breach_relevance: No IR team contributed to 7 day delay in notifying Rockstar

responses.question: Have you tested your incident response process?
responses.breach_relevance: Untested IR process failed when real breach occurred

responses.question: Do you have a business continuity plan?
responses.breach_relevance: April 4 connector outage had no recovery communication to customers

responses.question: Do you have a process to remediate new risks?
responses.breach_relevance: Token theft via SaaS integrations was a known pattern from prior ShinyHunters attacks
